skill.name: monitoring-observability
skill.description: Set up monitoring, logging, and observability for applications and infrastructure. Use when implementing health checks, metrics collection, log aggregation, or alerting systems. Handles Prometheus, Grafana, ELK Stack, Datadog, and monitoring best practices.
skill.summary: Set up monitoring, logging, and observability for applications using Prometheus, Grafana, and structured logging.
skill.author: supercent-io

security_audit.summary: This is a documentation skill containing example code for monitoring systems. All static findings are false positives: the detected patterns are legitimate code examples (TypeScript, YAML), standard Node.js environment variable usage for configuration, documentation links to known monitoring tools, and Prometheus query syntax. No actual security risks present.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Commands False Positive
dangerous_patterns.description: Static scanner detected 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' but this is TypeScript/YAML code examples in documentation, not actual shell commands. Lines 25-386 contain example code for Prometheus metrics, Winston logging, and alert configurations.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Code examples in documentation are not executable shell commands. The scanner misinterprets YAML and TypeScript syntax as backtick execution.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network Access False Positive
dangerous_patterns.description: Static scanner detected hardcoded URLs and IPs but these are legitimate: prometheus.io, grafana.com, Google SRE Book are official documentation links; '1.2.3.4' is an example IP in log output.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Reference URLs point to official monitoring tool documentation. Example IP is placeholder data in logging example.

dangerous_patterns.title: Environment Variable False Positive
dangerous_patterns.description: Static scanner detected env_access but process.env.LOG_LEVEL and process.env.NODE_ENV are standard Node.js configuration patterns, not sensitive credential access.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: These are standard, non-sensitive environment variables for logging configuration and environment identification.

dangerous_patterns.title: Path Traversal False Positive
dangerous_patterns.description: Static scanner detected path traversal but line 233 contains Prometheus query syntax (time range [5m]) and lines 382-383 contain relative documentation links between skill files.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Prometheus syntax [5m] is a time range, not a file path. Relative links are normal documentation references.

content.value_statement: This skill helps developers and DevOps engineers implement comprehensive monitoring, logging, and alerting systems using industry-standard tools like Prometheus and Grafana.
actual_capabilities: Set up Prometheus metrics collection with custom application instrumentation
Configure alerting rules for error rates, response times, and system health
Implement structured logging with Winston for Node.js applications
Create Grafana dashboards with Golden Signals (latency, traffic, errors, saturation)
Build health check endpoints with dependency status monitoring
limitations: Does not provide managed monitoring infrastructure (requires self-hosted or cloud Prometheus/Grafana)
Examples focus on Node.js/TypeScript; other languages require adaptation
Does not cover cloud-native monitoring solutions like AWS CloudWatch or GCP Monitoring

use_cases.title: Production Monitoring Setup
use_cases.description: Set up comprehensive monitoring before deploying applications to production

use_cases.title: Incident Response Readiness
use_cases.description: Create alerting rules and dashboards for quick root cause identification during incidents

use_cases.title: Performance Optimization
use_cases.description: Identify performance bottlenecks through metrics collection and visualization

prompt_templates.title: Basic Prometheus Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up Prometheus metrics collection for my Node.js Express application. Include default metrics and a custom histogram for HTTP request duration.

prompt_templates.title: Alert Configuration
prompt_templates.prompt: Create Prometheus alert rules for high error rate (5xx errors above 5%), slow response time (p95 above 1 second), and service downtime.

prompt_templates.title: Structured Logging
prompt_templates.prompt: Implement Winston logger with JSON format, timestamp, and error stack traces for my Node.js application.

prompt_templates.title: Health Check Endpoint
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a /health endpoint that checks database connectivity, Redis availability, and returns overall service status.

output_examples.input: Set up Prometheus metrics for my Express app
output_examples.output: Metrics endpoint configuration with custom histogram for request duration, counter for total requests, and default system metrics.

output_examples.input: Create alerts for high error rate
output_examples.output: Prometheus alert rule with 5-minute evaluation, 5% threshold for 5xx errors, critical severity label.

best_practices: Define clear Service Level Objectives (SLOs) before setting up alerts to prevent alert fatigue
Use high-cardinality labels carefully to avoid performance issues in Prometheus
Write runbooks for each alert to enable fast incident response
anti_patterns: Do not log sensitive data like passwords, API keys, or personally identifiable information
Avoid creating too many alerts which leads to alert fatigue and ignored notifications
Do not use high-cardinality labels like user IDs or session tokens in metrics

faq.question: What is the difference between metrics and logs?
faq.answer: Metrics are numerical measurements collected at intervals (like request rate), while logs are discrete events with detailed context. Use metrics for alerting and dashboards, logs for debugging specific issues.

faq.question: What are Golden Signals in monitoring?
faq.answer: Golden Signals are four key metrics: latency (response time), traffic (request volume), errors (error rate), and saturation (resource utilization). These provide comprehensive system health visibility.

faq.question: How do I prevent alert fatigue?
faq.answer: Only create alerts for actionable issues that require immediate response. Set appropriate thresholds and evaluation durations. Document runbooks for each alert.

faq.question: What is the Prometheus pull model?
faq.answer: Prometheus scrapes metrics from targets at configured intervals rather than having applications push data. This provides better reliability and simplifies application code.

faq.question: How do I monitor custom business metrics?
faq.answer: Use Prometheus client libraries to define custom counters, gauges, histograms, or summaries in your application code. Expose them via a /metrics endpoint.

faq.question: What should be included in a health check?
faq.answer: A health check should verify critical dependencies (database, cache, external APIs), return overall status (healthy/degraded/unhealthy), and include latency information for each check.
